introRows.html: Chance plays a vital role on the battlefield, as a well-aimed volley can harmlessly ricochet off reinforced armour, or a desperate last stand can turn the tide of an entire battle. In this Part, we’ll discuss how dice are used to resolve uncertain outcomes, from basic rolls and modifiers to the Roll-Off, which settles disputes and decides initiative.
introRows.plain: Chance plays a vital role on the battlefield, as a well-aimed volley can harmlessly ricochet off reinforced armour, or a desperate last stand can turn the tide of an entire battle. In this Part, we’ll discuss how dice are used to resolve uncertain outcomes, from basic rolls and modifiers to the Roll-Off, which settles disputes and decides initiative.

rows.html: The game uses standard six-sided dice (D6).
rows.plain: The game uses standard six-sided dice ( D6 ).

rows.html: D6: Roll one die. The result is the number shown.
rows.plain: D6: Roll one die. The result is the number shown.

rows.html: D3: Roll one D6 and halve the result, rounding up: 1–2 = 1, 3–4 = 2, 5–6 = 3.
rows.plain: D3: Roll one D6 and halve the result, rounding up: 1–2 = 1 , 3–4 = 2 , 5–6 = 3 .

rows.html: Multiple Dice (2D6, 3D6): Roll the stated number of dice and add the results together.
rows.plain: Multiple Dice (2D6, 3D6): Roll the stated number of dice and add the results together.

rows.html: When a rule calls for a Roll-Off, both players roll 2D6. The higher total wins. Ties are re-rolled until a winner is determined.
rows.plain: When a rule calls for a Roll-Off, both players roll 2D6. The higher total wins. Ties are re-rolled until a winner is determined.

rows.html: Some abilities grant the chance to re-roll, allowing a second attempt when the first result is unfavourable. Pick up the die and roll it again. The new result replaces the original, even if it is worse. Unless stated otherwise, a re-roll applies to a single die. If an ability specifies re-rolling multiple dice, follow its instructions.
rows.plain: Some abilities grant the chance to re-roll, allowing a second attempt when the first result is unfavourable. Pick up the die and roll it again. The new result replaces the original, even if it is worse. Unless stated otherwise, a re-roll applies to a single die. If an ability specifies re-rolling multiple dice , follow its instructions.

rows.html: Modifiers adjust the Target Number of a roll. Apply all modifiers before rolling.
rows.plain: Modifiers adjust the Target Number of a roll. Apply all modifiers before rolling.

rows.html: +X Modifier: Makes the roll easier, reducing the Target Number by X.
rows.plain: +X Modifier : Makes the roll easier, reducing the Target Number by X.

rows.html: -X Modifier: Makes the roll harder, increasing the Target Number by X.
rows.plain: -X Modifier : Makes the roll harder, increasing the Target Number by X.

rows.html: Modifiers from different named sources are cumulative unless otherwise stated.
rows.plain: Modifiers from different named sources are cumulative unless otherwise stated.

rows.html: EXAMPLE: A Marine needs 5+ to hit.
rows.plain: EXAMPLE: A Marine needs 5+ to hit.

rows.html: A +1 Modifier reduces the Target Number by 1. He now hits on 4+.
rows.plain: A +1 Modifier reduces the Target Number by 1. He now hits on 4+ .

rows.html: A -1 Modifier increases the Target Number by 1. He now hits on 6+.
rows.plain: A -1 Modifier increases the Target Number by 1. He now hits on 6+ .

rows.html: Ability text: " +1 to Armour" means the Armour Roll improves (Target Number decreases). " -1 to Hit" means hitting becomes harder (Target Number increases).
rows.plain: Ability text: " +1 to Armour" means the Armour Roll improves (Target Number decreases). " -1 to Hit" means hitting becomes harder (Target Number increases).

rows.html: A Target Number can never be modified below 2+ or above 6+.
rows.plain: A Target Number can never be modified below 2+ or above 6+.

rows.html: DESIGNER’S NOTE: Modifiers adjust the Target Number, not the dice roll itself. This keeps arithmetic simple, as a player always knows exactly what the Target Number is before rolling the dice. It also ensures that automatic successes on a roll of 6 and automatic failures on a roll of 1 are clear, no matter how many modifiers are applied to a single roll.
rows.plain: DESIGNER’S NOTE: Modifiers adjust the Target Number, not the dice roll itself. This keeps arithmetic simple, as a player always knows exactly what the Target Number is before rolling the dice . It also ensures that automatic successes on a roll of 6 and automatic failures on a roll of 1 are clear, no matter how many modifiers are applied to a single roll.

rows.html: If a rule says " D3+1" , roll the die and add 1 to the result. This is not a modifier to a Target Number - it generates a value (e. g. 4 Damage instead of 3).
rows.plain: If a rule says " D3+1" , roll the die and add 1 to the result. This is not a modifier to a Target Number - it generates a value (e. g. 4 Damage instead of 3).

rows.html: No amount of preparation guarantees a perfect outcome, and no situation is ever truly hopeless. The dice always leave room for the unexpected.
rows.plain: No amount of preparation guarantees a perfect outcome, and no situation is ever truly hopeless. The dice always leave room for the unexpected.

rows.html: Regardless of the final Target Number, a natural roll of 6 always succeeds, and a natural roll of 1 always fails. No modifier or ability can override these boundaries.
rows.plain: Regardless of the final Target Number, a natural roll of 6 always succeeds, and a natural roll of 1 always fails. No modifier or ability can override these boundaries.

rows.html: A Null Value (-) indicates that the capability does not exist. No modifier can grant a roll where a Null Value applies.
rows.plain: A Null Value (-) indicates that the capability does not exist. No modifier can grant a roll where a Null Value applies.

rows.html: Whenever the rules ask a player to roll against a number, an Armour Roll, a Hit roll, or an Evade check, that roll is a Test. Every Test follows the same logic: there is a Target Number, the player rolls, and the result either meets the threshold or it does not.
rows.plain: Whenever the rules ask a player to roll against a number, an Armour Roll , a Hit roll, or an Evade check, that roll is a Test. Every Test follows the same logic: there is a Target Number, the player rolls, and the result either meets the threshold or it does not.

rows.html: Tests come in two flavours:
rows.plain: Tests come in two flavours:

rows.html: Characteristic Tests use a value from the Unit’s own profile, its Armour, its Evade, and its native resilience. When a squad of Marines takes fire and rolls their Armour Rolls, that is a Characteristic Test.
rows.plain: Characteristic Tests use a value from the Unit’s own profile, its Armour, its Evade, and its native resilience. When a squad of Marines takes fire and rolls their Armour Rolls , that is a Characteristic Test.

rows.html: Attribute Tests use a value from a Weapon Profile or Special Rule, such as a weapon’s Hit characteristic. When those same Marines fire their C-14 Rifles and roll to hit, that is an Attribute Test.
rows.plain: Attribute Tests use a value from a Weapon Profile or Special Rule, such as a weapon’s Hit characteristic. When those same Marines fire their C-14 Rifles and roll to hit, that is an Attribute Test.

rows.html: Not everything involving dice is a Test. Value Generation - a Charge Roll, a D3 Damage result, simply produces a number. The player adds the die result to the base value as instructed. There is no Target Number to beat.
rows.plain: Not everything involving dice is a Test. Value Generation - a Charge Roll , a D3 Damage result, simply produces a number. The player adds the die result to the base value as instructed. There is no Target Number to beat.

rows.html: To resolve any Test: identify the base Target Number, apply all Modifiers to determine the final Target Number, and roll the die. If the result is equal to or exceeds the Target Number, the Test succeeds. If it falls short, the Test fails.
rows.plain: To resolve any Test: identify the base Target Number, apply all Modifiers to determine the final Target Number, and roll the die. If the result is equal to or exceeds the Target Number, the Test succeeds. If it falls short, the Test fails.

rows.html: If a die does not land flat on the playing surface, lodged against terrain, tilted on a base, or off the table entirely, the roll is invalid. Pick it up and roll it again. Players should agree before the game on what constitutes a cocked die. The simplest test: if the result isn' t obvious at a glance, re-roll it.
rows.plain: If a die does not land flat on the playing surface, lodged against terrain, tilted on a base, or off the table entirely, the roll is invalid. Pick it up and roll it again. Players should agree before the game on what constitutes a cocked die. The simplest test: if the result isn' t obvious at a glance, re-roll it.
